tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and proteinuria, indicating possible renal impairment. Urine findings (glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, acidic pH, brown color) suggest possible diabetic ketoacidosis or severe hyperglycemia with renal involvement. Mildly elevated ALT may indicate hepatic stress. Immediate clinical evaluation for acute kidney injury and metabolic derangements is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
